documentation: PyTorch Profiler TensorBoard Plugin :         https://github.com/pytorch/kineto/tree/main/tb_plugin

functions.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.utils.as_str_any
documentation:
Converts to `str` as `str(value)`, but use `as_str` for `bytes`.

Args:
value: A object that can be converted to `str`.

Returns:
A `str` object.

functions.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.utils.as_text
documentation:
Returns the given argument as a unicode string.

Args:
bytes_or_text: A `bytes`, `str`, or `unicode` object.
encoding: A string indicating the charset for decoding unicode.

Returns:
A `str` (Python 3) object.

Raises:
TypeError: If `bytes_or_text` is not a binary or unicode string.

functions.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.utils.as_bytes
documentation:
Converts either bytes or unicode to `bytes`, using utf-8 encoding for
text.

Args:
bytes_or_text: A `bytes`, `str`, or `unicode` object.
encoding: A string indicating the charset for encoding unicode.

Returns:
A `bytes` object.

Raises:
TypeError: If `bytes_or_text` is not a binary or unicode string.

methods.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.azureblob.AzureBlobSystem.split_blob_path
documentation:
Find the first blob start with blob_path, then get the relative path starting from dirname(blob_path).
Finally, split the relative path.
return (basename(blob_path), [relative splitted paths])
If blob_path doesn't exist, return (None, None)
For example,
    For blob https://trainingdaemon.blob.core.windows.net/tests/test1/test2/test.txt
    * If the blob_path is '', return ('', [test1, test2, test.txt])
    * If the blob_path is test1, return (test1, [test2, test.txt])
    * If the blob_path is test1/test2, return (test2, [test2, test.txt])
    * If the blob_path is test1/test2/test.txt, return (test.txt, [test.txt])

methods.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.azureblob.AzureBlobSystem.container_and_path
documentation: Split an Azure blob -prefixed URL into container and blob path.
Provides filesystem access to S3.

modules.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.cache
documentation: Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.

methods.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.cache.Cache.__getstate__
documentation:
The multiprocessing module can start one of three ways: spawn, fork, or forkserver.
The default mode is fork in Unix and spawn on Windows and macOS.
Therefore, the __getstate__ and __setstate__ are used to pickle/unpickle the state in spawn mode.

methods.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.cache.Cache.__setstate__
documentation:
The default logging level in new process is warning. Only warning and error log can be written to
streams.
So, we need call use_absl_handler in the new process.

methods.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.cache.Cache.get_remote_cache
documentation: Try to get the local file in the cache. download it to local if it cannot be found in cache.

modules.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.file
documentation:
This file is forked from
https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorboard/blob/master/tensorboard/compat/tensorflow_stub/io/gfile.py.
The following functionalities are added after forking:
* Check Azure Blob & Google Cloud available or not
* get_filesystem changes to support Azure Blobs
* add BaseFileSystem and PathBase abstracted class for the filesystem.
* add download_file for each file system to cache the remote file to local temporary folder.
* add AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY for S3 file system which is not supported by tensorboard.
* add Azure blob file system
* add Google Cloud file system
* add specialized walk for Local file system, Azure Blob and Google Cloud to improve the walk performance.
* add global wrapper for abspath, basename, join, download_file.
* change the global walk wrapper to support specialized walk.

documentation:
Returns a list of entries contained within a directory.

The list is in arbitrary order. It does not contain the special entries "."
and "..".

functions.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.file.makedirs
documentation: Creates a directory and all parent/intermediate directories.

functions.name: torch_tb_profiler.io.file.walk
documentation:
Recursive directory tree generator for directories.

Args:
  top: string, a Directory name
  topdown: bool, Traverse pre order if True, post order if False.
  onerror: optional handler for errors. Should be a function, it will be
    called with the error as argument. Rethrowing the error aborts the walk.

Errors that happen while listing directories are ignored.

Yields:
  Each yield is a 3-tuple:  the pathname of a directory, followed by lists
  of all its subdirectories and leaf files.
  (dirname, [subdirname, subdirname, ...], [filename, filename, ...])
  as strings

functions.name: torch_tb_profiler.utils._areas_of_triangles
documentation:
Calculate areas of triangles from duples of vertex coordinates.

Uses implicit numpy broadcasting along first axis of `bs`.

# Returns

numpy.array

: Array of areas of shape (len(bs),)

functions.name: torch_tb_profiler.utils.lttb_sample
documentation:
sample `memory_curves` to `n_out` points using the LTTB algorithm.

# Parameters

memory_curves

: A dict, key for device (cpu, gpu0, gpu1, ...),
  value is a list of list of (time,allocated,reverved)

n_out

: Number of data points to downsample to

# Returns

sumpled memory_curves with at most n_out points.

methods.name: torch_tb_profiler.profiler.op_tree.OpTreeBuilder._group_backward_nodes
documentation:
All nodes are backward nodes startswith autograd::engine::evaluate_function.
If one node's name is autograd::engine::evaluate_function: torch::autograd::AccumulateGrad,
it should be grouped with previous normal backward node. Otherwise, a new backward node should be started

Returns whether there is relevant data for the plugin to process.
If there is no any pending run, hide the plugin

methods.name: torch_tb_profiler.plugin.TorchProfilerPlugin._get_run_dirs
documentation:
Scan logdir, find PyTorch Profiler run directories.
A directory is considered to be a run if it contains 1 or more *.pt.trace.json[.gz].
E.g. there are 2 runs: run1, run2
    /run1
        /[worker1].pt.trace.json.gz
        /[worker2].pt.trace.json.gz
    /run2
        /[worker1].pt.trace.json
TensorBoard plugin for Torch Profiler.
